coredata.dc:title: Novel green synthesis of N-doped carbon dots from fruits of Opuntia ficus Indica as an effective catalyst for the photocatalytic degradation of methyl orange dye and antibacterial studies
coredata.dc:creator: Rajapandi, Subramani
Pandeeswaran, M.
Kousalya, G.N.
coredata.dc:description: A carbon dot is an attractive material for the photocatalytic degradation of organic pollutant. Moreover it’s extensively used as a material to control the growth of micro organisms. Herein we are reporting the synthesis of Undoped and N-doped carbon dots (N-CDs) derived from Opuntia Ficus Indica (O.F.Indica) fruits. Further we have well characterized by UV–vis, FT-IR, XPS spectral, HR-TEM, Raman and XRD techniques. The synthesized Undoped and N-CDs are stable for 3 months. Finally we have found the N-CDs shows the higher photocatalytic activity then Undoped C-dots, due to presence of N-atom. We also have discussed the possible degradation mechanism of Methyl Orange (MO). Finally the N-CDs was utilized their antibacterial activities. We used Streptococcus Pyogenes, Staphylococcus Aureus, Klebsiella Pneumoniae, and Shigella Flexneri. Among them we found Streptococcus Pyogenes a bacterium was largely controlled the growth by our N-CDs.
coredata.dcterms:subject: N-doped carbon dots
Opuntia ficus indica
Methyl orange
Anti-bacterial study
Dye degradation

xocs:item-toc-entry.xocs:item-toc-section-title: Synthesis of Undoped CDs and N-CDs
Characterization of Undoped CDs and N-CDs
UV–vis and emission spectral studies of Undoped and N-CDs
FT-IR spectral studies of NU and N-CDs
XRD pattern and Raman spectral studies of N-CDs
HR-TEM image and XPS study of the N-CDs
Photocatalytic dye degradation of methyl orange dye by Undoped and N-CDs
Anti-bacterial study of Undoped CDs and N-CDs

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: UV–vis spectrum of nitrogen doped C-dots. Inset: Photographs of N-doped C-dots under (left) day light and (right) UV-light.
HR-TEM image of N-doped C-dots. Inset: Particles size histogram.
XPS spectra of N-doped C-dots A) Survey B) C1s C) N1s D) O1s.
Photocatalytic degradation of MO dye using N-doped C-dots as catalyst with NaBH4 under different time interval.
A) Photocatalytic dye degradation efficiency percentage of N-doped C-dots with MO dye.
B) Kinetic plot for photocatalytic degradation of MO dye with N-CDs.

O.F.Indica N-Doped CDs involved in against human pathogens of A) Streptococcus Pyogenes [SP](MTCC442), B) Staphylococcus Aureus [SA](MTCC10787), C) Klebsiella Pneumoniae [KP](MTCC432), D) Shigella Flexneri [SF](MTCC1457) in different concentrations in 25 µg, 50 µg, 75 µg, 100 µg and Streptomycin sulphate as positive control in anti-bacterial agent.
Schematic representation for the photocatalytic degradation of MO using N-doped C-dots.
The zone of inhibition of Opuntia ficus Indica derived N-undoped and N-CDs were involved in gram positive and gram negative human pathogens of 4 different bacteria’s.

row.entry: Various concentration of Opuntia Ficus Indica Derived CDs (µg mL−1)

head.ce:title: Novel green synthesis of N-doped carbon dots from fruits of Opuntia ficus Indica as an effective catalyst for the photocatalytic degradation of methyl orange dye and antibacterial studies

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: PG and Research Department of Chemistry, GTN Arts College (Autonomous), Dindigul 624 005, Tamil Nadu, India

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: PG and Research Department of Chemistry

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: PG and Research Department of Chemistry, GTN Arts College (Autonomous), Dindigul 624 005, Tamil Nadu, India

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: A carbon dot is an attractive material for the photocatalytic degradation of organic pollutant. Moreover it’s extensively used as a material to control the growth of micro organisms. Herein we are reporting the synthesis of Undoped and N-doped carbon dots (N-CDs) derived from Opuntia Ficus Indica (O.F.Indica) fruits. Further we have well characterized by UV–vis, FT-IR, XPS spectral, HR-TEM, Raman and XRD techniques. The synthesized Undoped and N-CDs are stable for 3 months. Finally we have found the N-CDs shows the higher photocatalytic activity then Undoped C-dots, due to presence of N-atom. We also have discussed the possible degradation mechanism of Methyl Orange (MO). Finally the N-CDs was utilized their antibacterial activities. We used Streptococcus Pyogenes, Staphylococcus Aureus, Klebsiella Pneumoniae, and Shigella Flexneri. Among them we found Streptococcus Pyogenes a bacterium was largely controlled the growth by our N-CDs.

ce:data-availability.ce:para: Data will be made available on request.

ce:section.ce:para: Water pollution is a global issue in particularly dye is received considerable attention. Organic dyes are extensively used in pharmaceuticals, paper, rubber, textile, leather, cosmetics, plastics and food industries [1]. Organic dyes are considering a wide variety of pollutants in the natural water sources are water treatment systems. Organic dyes are harmful to the living beings, moreover it can cause the carcinogenic, majority of the organic dyes are soluble in organic solvents, the organic solvents also living beings. In particularly methyl orange (MO) dye is widely used in the textile industry [2]. The MO is toxic nature and it’s harmful for environment, organisms. Hence, the treatment of waste water is highly essential today. There are many methods available for wastewater treatment, including reverse osmosis, electrochemical porosis and sedimentation method, filtration method, photocatalytic method [2].Among them photocatalytic degradation method is received much attention due to their fast degradation and less toxic, cost effective etc., [3].

Generally the photocatalytic degradation method is a useful tool for the degradation of dyes, in the presence of UV or visible light using sodium borohydride (NaBH4) as reducing agent. Sodium borohydride in the absence of catalyst is kinetically difficult but thermodynamically favorable. Hence we have to use the nanoparticles as a catalyst and it is alternative path by the reduction of the activation energy. There by decreasing the kinetic barriers making kinetically favor [3,4]. An effective catalyst with an inter mediate redox potential between the acceptor species an electron donors can help in the transfer of electrons which can act as an electron relay system [4,5].

Carbon dots (CDs) are a new class of carbon nanomaterials Size less than 10 nm in diameter [6]. CDs are received attracted attention by the scientist in the recent years because of the fascinating physical and chemical properties [7]. CDs are an alternative florescent material for dyes. CDs are zero dimensional materials with relatively strong fluorescence characters [8]. CDs are bio-degradable, non toxic and we can easy functionalize the excitation dependent fluorescence in a quantum yield. There are many methods available for the synthesis of CDs, which are includes laser ablation [9], acid oxidation [10], hydrothermal [11], microwave irradiations [12], electro chemical synthesis [13] etc. Generally many people have chosen the hydrothermal method to synthesis CDs, Bottom-up approaches are easy, cheap, and environmentally beneficial. For the creation of CDs, the hydrothermal-carbonization approach in particular is straightforward, widely applicable, and has a constrained size distribution. The fruit biomass, Vegetables, milk, caramel, honey have all been used as sources of green carbon for the synthesis of CDs. CDs are also prepared by natural precursors such as plants, roots, leaves, fruits and waste materials were used as carbon source. According to recent research, N-CDs made from plant extract from the Borassus flabellifer plant increase catalytic activity while lowering the organic moiety of the dye Congo-red [14]. N-CDs produced from Cyamopsis tetragonoloba plant extract were used to investigate the electro catalytic activity [15]. N-CDs from the Luffa acutangula plant extract were reported in Quercetin as an anti-cancer substance, and N-CDs served as a drug carrier [16]. Morinda citrifolia plant extract derived Pd-NCDs composite material electro catalytic activities are in advanced investigations of this CDs era [17,18]. This type of biogenic synthesis comes under the category of bottom-up approach synthesis. On the other hand, the heavy metal doped CDs has provide to be an effective root to improve the quantum yield in making high stability [2]. In particularly, the N-CDs have improved the quantum yield and both amine and carboxyl groups benefits the high stable CDs. Further, the CDs also extensively used for the antibacterial activities. Carbon materials have a capacity to control the growth of microorganisms. In particularly the various bacterial and fungal diseases could be controlled by CDs. Moreover, these antibacterial studies can help to develop antibiotics drugs and antibacterial ingredients.

It is desirable to develop biogenic CDs from natural plants and waste materials and apply them for antibacterial activities study and environmental dye degradation applications. We kept these objectives in our mind, and chosen Opuntia species as carbon source to synthesis CDs. These Opuntia ficus Indica was used as a material for the treatment of chronic diseases such as diabetes, obesity, cardiovascular diseases and cancer [19]. Further, these fruits are rich source of alkaloids, terpenoids, flavanoids and carotenes. This Opuntia ficus Indica play a major role for the bioreductions inorganic materials [20].

We have used Opuntia Ficus Indica for the synthesis of CDs by hydrothermal method. Additionally, we have prepared Nitrogen doped CDs (N-CDs) using liquid ammonia along with Opuntia Ficus Indica by hydrothermal method. Herein, we are reporting the Opuntia ficus Indica derived Carbon dots synthesis based on our knowledge, Opuntia ficus Indica fruits derived carbon dots by hydrothermal method is a first report. Generally hydrothermal method is more useful than other methodology, within 8 h 180°c moreover green fluorescence particles has been reported. Hence we conclude our methodology is new and better than others reported earlier. The synthesis and characterization of Undoped and N-doped CDs Opuntia Ficus Indica and their characterization by UV–vis, FT-IR spectral, XRD, XPS, and HR-TEM image techniques. The synthesized N-doped and Undoped CDs are stable for more than three months, which shows the emission maximum at 440 and 473 nm respectively. The color of the solution becomes green while we irradiate the UV light. Among the N-doped and Undoped CDs we found N-doped CDs shows the higher catalytic activities than the Undoped CDs. Hence we have used N-CDs as a catalyst for the photocatalytic degradation of MO dye in environmental water and also we have been applied this N-doped CDs as an antibacterial agents for the controlling of the growth of Streptococcus Pyogenes, Staphylococcus Aureus, Klebsiella Pneumoniae, and Shigella Flexneri. We have observed N-CDs were acted as good antibacterial agent to control the growth of the bacteria.

Aqueous Ammonia (Aq.NH3) (25 %), Methyl orange dye and NaBH4 was purchased from Sigma-Aldrich. Double distilled (DD) water was used in this work. Phosphate buffer saline was used in antimicrobial study; antibiotic mixture of streptomycin was also purchased from Merck. The gram positive and gram negative bacteria’s were purchased from GK Biochemical. The dialysis tube (Fisher Scientific) diameter size was (MWCO) 3.5 kDa and 29.3 mm.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Synthesis of Undoped CDs and N-CDs
ce:section.ce:para: The O.F.Indica derived Carbon dots synthesized from hydrothermal method. The O.F.Indica was handpicked by Western Ghats in Dindigul hills. The DD water and Aq.NH3 was used in this preparation of Nitrogen doped Carbon dots. The hydrothermal synthesis of CDs was prepared in two ways, like Undoped and N-Doped CDs (N-CD). The above CDs was prepared hydrothermally like, 25 g flesh of O.F.Indica was taken as a source material of CDs. Initially, it was mixed with 50 ml of double distilled water and added in the Teflon coated autoclave. Then the autoclave was placed in hot air oven at 200 °C for 8 h. After the reaction time, auto clave was taken out from oven. The solution was filtered in Whatman filter paper and color of the solution was observed in yellowish white. The filtered material of CDs was stored in 40c for future characterization. We were followed the same way for N-CDs, here in Aq.NH3 was added as a doping source of N-CDs.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Characterization of Undoped CDs and N-CDs
ce:section.ce:para: The characterization study of Undoped CDs and N-CDs was done accordingly. As a preliminary characterization, UV–vis and Photoluminescence spectrum was observed by JASCO V-750 spectrometer and JASCO V-8500 spectro-fluorometer accordingly. The stability study was observed in spectro-fluorometer in the respective time difference. The functional group of CDs was identified by FT-IR technique in the instrument of Jasco-FT-I460 plus spectrophotometer. The XRD and Raman spectral were analyzed by PANalytical X’pert diffractometer and STR 500 nm focal length laser Raman spectrometer respectively. The surface morphology and particle size of the NCDs was surveilled by FEI- TECNAI T20 G2 HR-TEM instrument and XPS scanning was taken as PHI 5000 Versa Probe III; instrument was used at a scan rate of 20 eV for One hour. The Photocatalytic degradation of MO-dye were monitored by JASCO V-750 spectrometer and anti-bacterial study of the human pathogens of Gram negative and positive were as examined in biological laboratory in Gandhigram Rural Institute, Gandhigram, Dindigul, Tamilnadu.

ce:section.ce:section-title: UV–vis and emission spectral studies of Undoped and N-CDs

ce:section.ce:para: It is very important to know about the stability of nanoparticles. We have performed the photo stability study of N-doped CDs by the continuous irradiation of UV-light up to 8 h. Even though the continuous irradiation of UV-light there is no changes in the fluorescence intensity. Hence the particle highly photo stable Figs. S5 & S6. We have systematically monitored their stability by fluorescence spectral changes. Further, we have studied the fluorescence spectral changes of CDs under different time period with the freshly prepared sample. Interestingly we haven’t seen any significant fluorescence intensity changes and emission maximum shift of both Undoped CDs (Fig. S5) and N-Doped (Fig. S6) CDs [22,23]. The sample of Undoped and N-Doped CDs was stored at 4 °C for 3 months, and the emission's stability and intensity were assessed using a UV–vis spectrophotometer. In basic state, the pH of the CDs solution is 10.

ce:section.ce:section-title: FT-IR spectral studies of NU and N-CDs
ce:section.ce:para: The FT-IR spectra of Undoped (Fig. S3) and Doped (Fig. S4) CDs shows the CO, Alkyne, OH/NH functionality groups which are at the corresponding peak positions of 1636, 2139, 3312 cm−1 and N-doped Carbon dots shows the CC, CC, Alkyne, CH, OH/NH functional groups, at the corresponding peak positions 1119, 1636, 2895, 3327 cm−1. The CO stretching frequency observed at 1542 cm−1 [24,25]. These results confirm that the presence of OH/NH and CO, hydroxyl, carboxyl group whereas N-doped shows the nitrogen functionalities.

ce:section.ce:section-title: XRD pattern and Raman spectral studies of N-CDs
ce:section.ce:para: Further, we have characterized the surface defect using Raman spectral techniques. The Raman spectrum of N-CDs was shows d-band at 1362 cm-1and G-band at1580 cm−1. The obtained G-band and d-band peaks are confirmed that the successful formation of N-CDs. We have measured the intensity ratio of D and G bands (ID/IG) and we got the intensity ratio as 0.82 which are confirming that the surface defect of the CDs. The origin of the fluorescence nature of the CDs is due to this surface defect [28].

ce:section.ce:section-title: HR-TEM image and XPS study of the N-CDs
ce:section.ce:para: The structural morphology and dispersity monitored by high resolution transmission electron microscopy. HR-TEM image of N-CDs shown in Fig. 5 . The obtained particles are in spherical shape. The average size of the particle was calculated by using image J software. The average diameter was found to be 4.76 ± 0.91 nm.

We have characterized the energy dispersive spectrum (Fig. 6 ) of elemental analysis, these results confirms that the presence of C, N, O and Cu it’s from grids [27]. The composition of the species is C (61.58 %), N (6.08 %) and O (32.34 %).

ce:section.ce:section-title: Photocatalytic dye degradation of methyl orange dye by Undoped and N-CDs
ce:section.ce:para: Finally, the synthesized N-doped and Undoped CDs were applied for the environmental applications in particularly the dye degradation application. Interestingly, UV–vis spectrum of Methyl orange shows the peak at 465 nm. After mixing with Undoped CDs and NaBH4 the peak at 465 nm was dramatically quenched with 5 min difference and after 30 min the peak was completely vanished. The photographs also show the color changes of methyl orange to colorless. The total degradation time for Methyl Orange dye by Undoped CDs was observed and found to be 40 min which was more time consuming and the same we can observe by percentage dye degradation graph. The degradation efficiency was found to be 100 % in 40 min time difference. Whereas, in the case of N-CDs as a catalyst for Methyl orange dye and NaBH4 act as reducing agent the time consumption was drastically reduced. Interestingly peak obtained at 465 nm was quenched after 1 min. When we increase the timing from 2 to 12 min, the intensity was dramatically quenched then finally it was vanished. We also plotted in lnAt /A0 vs time. More over us got photo degradation efficiency as 100 %.

We have proposed possible mechanism for the photocatalytic degradation of methyl orange using Undoped and N-CDs. The mechanism of methyl orange reduction by N-CDs is explained by the electron relay effect. On the surface of the NCDs, methyl orange (acceptor) and BH4 (donor) from NaBH4 were adsorbed. The adsorption process could be aided by the surface of NCDs that have significant functional groups, such as hydroxyl, due to the donor and accepter's electrostatic affinity. Although the redox potential difference between the donor and acceptor are somewhat high, the synthesized N-CDs have an intermediate reduction potential that aids in the transport of electrons from the donor (BH4 -) to the acceptor (methyl orange). Then, as the methyl orange gains the electron from the N-CDs surface and becomes reduced, the BH4 transfers electrons to the catalyst's surface. Here, the azo group (NN) is converted to the amine group when methyl orange is reduced by NaBH4. This demonstrates that synthesized N-CDs have an impact on NaBH4 catalytic activity throughout the methyl orange degradation process [30–32]. Then, as the methyl orange gains the electron from the N-CDs surface and becomes reduced, the BH4 transfers electrons to the catalyst's surface. Here, the azo group (NN) is converted to the amine group when methyl orange is reduced by NaBH4. This demonstrates that synthesized N-CDs have an impact on NaBH4 catalytic activity throughout the methyl orange degradation process. The electron transfer between the dye molecules and CDs is exhibited. NaBH4 act as a reducing agent to get a fragmentation of azo (NN) bonds [33]. The activity of energy is decreased due to the inherent actions of CDs. Eventually the dye molecule was reconstructed to the CO2 and H2O at the end; it leads to easy decomposition of methyl orange. The degradation efficiency was calculated by using lnAt/A0 vs time of kinetic theory of pseudo first order kinetics, in this reduction thermodynamically and kinetically favorable at the same time catalyst of Undoped CD and N-CDs were accelerate the rate of the reaction [34]. The Scheme 1 shows the schematic representation of the degradation of methyl orange using N-CDs. The also have performing the Undoped carbon dots, but N-doped carbon dots shown the high photocatalytic activities due to the presence of Nitrogen. When we do the doping there is chance to enhance the band gap. Hence their performance is good [35].

ce:section.ce:section-title: Anti-bacterial study of Undoped CDs and N-CDs
ce:section.ce:para: Finally we have applied our synthesized CDs for anti-bacterial studies and streptomycin is a control of the antimicrobial study, the well size is 6 mm. In this methodology we have followed a very famous and facile technique of Agar-diffusion disk method for antibacterial strains. These bacteria’s are known as Gram positive and Gram negative bacteria’s were as involved in this study. The Fig. 11 . 1) Streptococcus Pyogenes, 2) Staphylococcus Aureus, 3) Klebsiella Pneumoniae, 4) Shigella Flexneri bacteria’s. Interestingly N-Undoped CDs (Fig. S11.1) Streptococcus Pyogenes, 2)Staphylococcus Aureus, 3)Klebsiella Pneumoniae, 4) Shigella Flexneri bacteria’s use different concentrations of CDs for the zone of inhibition study from 25 µg mL−1 to100 µg mL−1 we have been taken and we used Streptococcus Pyogenes, Staphylococcus Aureus, Klebsiella Pneumoniae, Shigella Flexneri bacteria’s. Only we have been food the material has been killed the bacteria’s. Whereas, in the case of N-CDs we have been observed it was very fast and also kills the large number of bacteria. The highest result of the zone of inhibition is 100 µg mL−1 in both CDs. Table 1 shows the zone of inhibition in gram negative and gram positive bacterial species.

Kang and Bandosz etal, the anti-bacterial study mechanism of action of CDs was demonstrated in S and N-CDs, it increase in the repulsive force between the less positively charged N-CDs and negatively charged bacterial surface of the human pathogens. The formation of different band gaps between the N-CDs as a result of fundamental and excited state in abate the highly reactive oxygen species (ROS) formation and diminish the bactericidal action in the respective application [36].

We have successfully synthesized Undoped and N-doped CDs from O.F.Indica by hydrothermal method. Then, it was characterized by FT-IR, EDS and XPS techniques, which were confirmed that the formation of CDs. The Raman spectrum of the N-CDs was confirming the graphitized nature. Fluorescence spectrum of the Undoped and N-CDs were as emit the excellent fluorescence in 440 nm and 473 nm, respectively. The HR-TEM image was confirms the C-Dots formation, size of the particle and amorphous nature of the particle. The catalytic activity of Undoped and N-CDs reduction of methyl orange dye were monitored by UV–Visible spectrophotometer. The degradation mechanism also was discussed. Further, anti-bacterial activity of the Undoped-CDs and N-CDs were studied using gram positive and gram negative bacteria. From our knowledge of literature this is the first time report of Nitrogen doped carbon dots, undoped carbon dots by using O.F India plant fruits and characterization studies.

ce:conflict-of-interest.ce:para: The authors declare that they have no known competing financial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper.
